best_bets.best_bet_raw: LAD ML (-205) 3 units — edge 9.9 pts over market, strong pitcher mismatch, no data integrity flags, and highest raw edge among my 3-unit plays.
best_bets.why_this: Sheehan’s elite SIERA and LAD’s top offense create a 75.9% true win probability against a fadeable Brandon Young; market still offers value at -205 despite the heavy favorite price.

Market pricing has overvalued Zack Wheeler, allowing us to back a solid David Peterson (3.91 xFIP) and a fully rested Mets bullpen at a huge plus-money price against a Phillies offense that struggles against left-handed pitching (92 wRC+).
Highest conviction edge from elite starter metrics vs weak small-sample opponent, with total also qualifying under independent run-gap rule.

The combination of Peterson's highly hittable profile (88 Stf+), both lineups generating elite hard-hit and barrel rates, and Citizens Bank Park's hitter-friendly dimensions pushes my projected total to 10.8 runs, creating an overwhelming 2.8-run gap against the market line.

Wheeler is pitching at an elite level (blended xFIP 3.07) facing a Peterson who has surrendered 14 earned runs in his last 18.1 innings with no L14 data to suggest recovery; the RL at plus-money (+110) means I'm getting paid over even money to back a team I estimate covers ~58% of the time, producing the widest gap on the slate at 10.4 percentage points with two independent model components supporting the edge.
